content: Existing LLM-based AI-agent frameworks can be augmented by integrating the 2025 iteration of the ICM curiosity mechanism to replace static, human-designed task repertoires with self-directed exploratory goal generation, where intrinsic reward from novel unstructured prompts or untested tool combinations drives agents to discover new use cases their original framework designers never explicitly coded. This creates a self-improving cycle where agents curate new role definitions and composable workflows that can be contributed back to the open-source framework ecosystem.
